channel.title: Robots Radio Briefings
channel.description: Daily briefings and deep-dives from Robots Radio.
channel.itunes:author: Robots Radio

item.title: Records, Revisions and Revenue: The Week the Energy Transition Got Serious About Standards
item.description: From a landmark UK renewables generation record to a long-awaited overhaul of corporate net-zero accounting, this Tuesday's briefing is dense with signals for practitioners. Battery storage is maturing fast, France is swinging big on offshore wind, and the frameworks that underpin how we measure all of it are quietly being rewritten.
item.itunes:title: Records, Revisions and Revenue: The Week the Energy Transition Got Serious About Standards
item.itunes:summary: From a landmark UK renewables generation record to a long-awaited overhaul of corporate net-zero accounting, this Tuesday's briefing is dense with signals for practitioners. Battery storage is maturing fast, France is swinging big on offshore wind, and the frameworks that underpin how we measure all of it are quietly being rewritten.

item.title: The Gene That Launched a Thousand Frameworks
item.description: Fifty years ago, Richard Dawkins reframed evolution around the gene rather than the organism — and the ripples never stopped. From behavioural economics to cultural theory, we trace why a slim Oxford paperback remains one of the most contested and consequential books in modern intellectual history.
item.itunes:title: The Gene That Launched a Thousand Frameworks
item.itunes:summary: Fifty years ago, Richard Dawkins reframed evolution around the gene rather than the organism — and the ripples never stopped. From behavioural economics to cultural theory, we trace why a slim Oxford paperback remains one of the most contested and consequential books in modern intellectual history.

item.title: Storage surges, buildings brace, and the auction machine keeps turning
item.description: From a landmark UK building efficiency consultation to a global battery storage boom, this Monday brings a cluster of signals that the energy transition is accelerating on multiple fronts simultaneously. The week opens with consolidation in Chinese solar, fresh European auction results, and some quietly important UK policy moves worth watching closely.
item.itunes:title: Storage surges, buildings brace, and the auction machine keeps turning
item.itunes:summary: From a landmark UK building efficiency consultation to a global battery storage boom, this Monday brings a cluster of signals that the energy transition is accelerating on multiple fronts simultaneously. The week opens with consolidation in Chinese solar, fresh European auction results, and some quietly important UK policy moves worth watching closely.

item.title: Deep Dive — 2026-04-05
item.itunes:title: Deep Dive — 2026-04-05

item.title: Fix It Again: How the Right to Repair is Rewiring Britain's Supply Chains
item.description: The UK's right to repair movement is quietly forcing manufacturers to rethink everything from component sourcing to product architecture. This morning we trace the legislative push, the modular design pioneers showing the way, and ask what a 1950s housewife selling Tupperware in a suburban living room can teach us about building products that last.
item.itunes:title: Fix It Again: How the Right to Repair is Rewiring Britain's Supply Chains
item.itunes:summary: The UK's right to repair movement is quietly forcing manufacturers to rethink everything from component sourcing to product architecture. This morning we trace the legislative push, the modular design pioneers showing the way, and ask what a 1950s housewife selling Tupperware in a suburban living room can teach us about building products that last.

item.title: The Sound of Old Wood: What Medieval Builders Knew About Acoustics That We're Only Now Remembering
item.description: A quiet revolution is underway in heritage conservation, as acousticians discover that the timber-framed buildings of the medieval world weren't just structurally clever — they were acoustically intentional. Retrofitting these structures for modern use is proving far more complicated than anyone expected, and the reasons why are forcing a fundamental rethink of what 'good sound' actually means.
item.itunes:title: The Sound of Old Wood: What Medieval Builders Knew About Acoustics That We're Only Now Remembering
item.itunes:summary: A quiet revolution is underway in heritage conservation, as acousticians discover that the timber-framed buildings of the medieval world weren't just structurally clever — they were acoustically intentional. Retrofitting these structures for modern use is proving far more complicated than anyone expected, and the reasons why are forcing a fundamental rethink of what 'good sound' actually means.

item.title: The 20% Solution: What Birdsong Teaches Us About Buildings That Actually Work
item.description: A new study confirms that listening to birdsong cuts cortisol levels by roughly a fifth — and the implications reach far beyond your morning walk. For anyone designing, leasing, or advising on commercial space, the science is starting to make a compelling economic case that nature-based office design changes how buildings perform, not just how they feel.
item.itunes:title: The 20% Solution: What Birdsong Teaches Us About Buildings That Actually Work
item.itunes:summary: A new study confirms that listening to birdsong cuts cortisol levels by roughly a fifth — and the implications reach far beyond your morning walk. For anyone designing, leasing, or advising on commercial space, the science is starting to make a compelling economic case that nature-based office design changes how buildings perform, not just how they feel.

item.title: Records, Rounds and Rewiring: The Week's Energy Headlines
item.description: UK renewables notch a new generation record as storage markets continue their global sprint. Meanwhile, the week's auction and financing news underlines just how much capital is now chasing clean energy infrastructure — from Ontario to Osaka.
item.itunes:title: Records, Rounds and Rewiring: The Week's Energy Headlines
item.itunes:summary: UK renewables notch a new generation record as storage markets continue their global sprint. Meanwhile, the week's auction and financing news underlines just how much capital is now chasing clean energy infrastructure — from Ontario to Osaka.

item.title: Carbon Physics, Storage Deals and a Record for Renewables: Thursday Briefing
item.description: Climate disclosure rules are turning accountants into amateur physicists, while the SBTi's carbon accounting framework quietly drifts from the emissions reality it was meant to track. Meanwhile, European battery storage markets are maturing fast — and the numbers from IRENA suggest the energy transition has found a second gear.
item.itunes:title: Carbon Physics, Storage Deals and a Record for Renewables: Thursday Briefing
item.itunes:summary: Climate disclosure rules are turning accountants into amateur physicists, while the SBTi's carbon accounting framework quietly drifts from the emissions reality it was meant to track. Meanwhile, European battery storage markets are maturing fast — and the numbers from IRENA suggest the energy transition has found a second gear.

item.title: Storage surges, standards shift, and the grid gets political
item.description: Europe's battery storage market is moving fast — from financing forums to grid fee frameworks — while the UK quietly reshapes its building efficiency rules. Meanwhile, geopolitical pressure from the Middle East is sending fresh ripples through energy markets, and global renewables capacity hit a new record.
item.itunes:title: Storage surges, standards shift, and the grid gets political
item.itunes:summary: Europe's battery storage market is moving fast — from financing forums to grid fee frameworks — while the UK quietly reshapes its building efficiency rules. Meanwhile, geopolitical pressure from the Middle East is sending fresh ripples through energy markets, and global renewables capacity hit a new record.

item.title: Nature's Balance Sheet: When Biodiversity Credits Meet Carbon Reality
item.description: Today's deep-dive asks whether the financialisation of nature through biodiversity credits is genuinely reshaping corporate carbon accounting — or simply adding a new layer of complexity to metrics that already struggle to reflect what is happening on the ground. We also scan a busy day in energy markets, from Spain and Italy setting solar records to the battery storage financing debate playing out in Barcelona.
item.itunes:title: Nature's Balance Sheet: When Biodiversity Credits Meet Carbon Reality
item.itunes:summary: Today's deep-dive asks whether the financialisation of nature through biodiversity credits is genuinely reshaping corporate carbon accounting — or simply adding a new layer of complexity to metrics that already struggle to reflect what is happening on the ground. We also scan a busy day in energy markets, from Spain and Italy setting solar records to the battery storage financing debate playing out in Barcelona.

item.title: Battery boom, Arctic sun, and the standards that shape your work
item.description: Europe's energy storage market is scaling fast and finding its financing footing, while solar keeps breaking records in surprising places. For consultants with UK and Iberian exposure, there's also meaningful movement on the standards and regulations that underpin portfolio decisions.
item.itunes:title: Battery boom, Arctic sun, and the standards that shape your work
item.itunes:summary: Europe's energy storage market is scaling fast and finding its financing footing, while solar keeps breaking records in surprising places. For consultants with UK and Iberian exposure, there's also meaningful movement on the standards and regulations that underpin portfolio decisions.

item.title: Carbon's Accidental Architecture — and the Battery Arms Race Reshaping Every Grid From Chile to New South Wales
item.description: Today's briefing takes the long view on carbon markets, tracing how a post-communist soap factory became the unlikely godfather of the voluntary credits your clients are buying right now. We also cover a remarkable week in battery storage, from a 700 Wh/kg chemistry breakthrough to a 2.6 GWh Chilean deal and a fresh 16 GWh Italian auction that will matter enormously for your Iberian modelling peers next door.
item.itunes:title: Carbon's Accidental Architecture — and the Battery Arms Race Reshaping Every Grid From Chile to New South Wales
item.itunes:summary: Today's briefing takes the long view on carbon markets, tracing how a post-communist soap factory became the unlikely godfather of the voluntary credits your clients are buying right now. We also cover a remarkable week in battery storage, from a 700 Wh/kg chemistry breakthrough to a 2.6 GWh Chilean deal and a fresh 16 GWh Italian auction that will matter enormously for your Iberian modelling peers next door.

item.title: Storage pipelines, scope 2 rewrites and the grid's growing ambitions
item.description: From a landmark Italian storage auction to the GHG Protocol's first Scope 2 overhaul in a decade, Tuesday brings a cluster of stories that matter for anyone modelling energy markets or advising clients on corporate climate commitments. There is also an intriguing technology subplot involving 700 Wh/kg lithium cells and a flywheel startup that refuses to be ignored.
item.itunes:title: Storage pipelines, scope 2 rewrites and the grid's growing ambitions
item.itunes:summary: From a landmark Italian storage auction to the GHG Protocol's first Scope 2 overhaul in a decade, Tuesday brings a cluster of stories that matter for anyone modelling energy markets or advising clients on corporate climate commitments. There is also an intriguing technology subplot involving 700 Wh/kg lithium cells and a flywheel startup that refuses to be ignored.

item.title: The post-handover blind spot: how UK building regs are letting developers off the carbon hook
item.description: Today's deep-dive unpacks the quiet loophole in the UK's latest building performance regulations that lets developers walk away from operational carbon responsibility the moment they hand over the keys. We also catch up on a busy weekend in energy storage, offshore wind, and Iberian market moves that your clients will want to know about.
item.itunes:title: The post-handover blind spot: how UK building regs are letting developers off the carbon hook
item.itunes:summary: Today's deep-dive unpacks the quiet loophole in the UK's latest building performance regulations that lets developers walk away from operational carbon responsibility the moment they hand over the keys. We also catch up on a busy weekend in energy storage, offshore wind, and Iberian market moves that your clients will want to know about.

item.title: Wind records, building rules and the long road to bankable storage
item.description: Britain breaks a wind generation record on the same weekend the government finally confirms its Future Homes and Buildings Standards, while the long-duration storage sector wrestles with how to move from promising chemistry to credible project finance. A busy Monday morning in the energy transition.
item.itunes:title: Wind records, building rules and the long road to bankable storage
item.itunes:summary: Britain breaks a wind generation record on the same weekend the government finally confirms its Future Homes and Buildings Standards, while the long-duration storage sector wrestles with how to move from promising chemistry to credible project finance. A busy Monday morning in the energy transition.

item.title: Deep Dive — 2026-03-29
item.itunes:title: Deep Dive — 2026-03-29

item.title: The Carbon We Can't See: How Embodied Emissions Are Reshaping the Built Environment
item.description: The carbon released before a building ever switches on a light is fast becoming the defining challenge of climate policy in the built environment. From concrete's inconvenient chemistry to a growing regulatory push on whole-life carbon, Sunday's briefing explores why the demolish-or-retrofit question is no longer just an architectural one — it's a carbon accounting crisis with real consequences for your clients' project pipelines.
item.itunes:title: The Carbon We Can't See: How Embodied Emissions Are Reshaping the Built Environment
item.itunes:summary: The carbon released before a building ever switches on a light is fast becoming the defining challenge of climate policy in the built environment. From concrete's inconvenient chemistry to a growing regulatory push on whole-life carbon, Sunday's briefing explores why the demolish-or-retrofit question is no longer just an architectural one — it's a carbon accounting crisis with real consequences for your clients' project pipelines.

item.title: Saturday Dispatch: Ancient Scripts, Crumbling Institutions, and the Price of Everything
item.description: This morning we take a detour through Neal Stephenson's Snow Crash and what cuneiform can teach us about language, power, and the architecture of reality. Along the way: NASA's space station mess, Apple's quiet retreat from the Mac Pro, and why AV1's royalty-free promise may be less solid than Silicon Valley would like you to believe.
item.itunes:title: Saturday Dispatch: Ancient Scripts, Crumbling Institutions, and the Price of Everything
item.itunes:summary: This morning we take a detour through Neal Stephenson's Snow Crash and what cuneiform can teach us about language, power, and the architecture of reality. Along the way: NASA's space station mess, Apple's quiet retreat from the Mac Pro, and why AV1's royalty-free promise may be less solid than Silicon Valley would like you to believe.

item.title: Wind records, battery booms, and the quiet rewiring of the energy system
item.description: Britain breaks its wind generation record and hits 22 GW of solar, while a global surge in battery storage investment reshapes everything from AI data centres to community energy. Meanwhile, the frameworks underpinning how we measure and price carbon risk are quietly being rebuilt from the ground up.
item.itunes:title: Wind records, battery booms, and the quiet rewiring of the energy system
item.itunes:summary: Britain breaks its wind generation record and hits 22 GW of solar, while a global surge in battery storage investment reshapes everything from AI data centres to community energy. Meanwhile, the frameworks underpinning how we measure and price carbon risk are quietly being rebuilt from the ground up.

item.title: Pension Capital, Fiduciary Duty, and the Quiet Rewiring of Britain's Energy Future
item.description: From landmark CfD contracts to Chinese turbine bans and a Scottish nacelle factory, today's news is thick with signals about who is building the UK's clean energy infrastructure — and who is paying for it. Beneath the headlines, a deeper story is emerging about how institutional capital is reshaping what gets built, where, and on whose terms.
item.itunes:title: Pension Capital, Fiduciary Duty, and the Quiet Rewiring of Britain's Energy Future
item.itunes:summary: From landmark CfD contracts to Chinese turbine bans and a Scottish nacelle factory, today's news is thick with signals about who is building the UK's clean energy infrastructure — and who is paying for it. Beneath the headlines, a deeper story is emerging about how institutional capital is reshaping what gets built, where, and on whose terms.

item.title: Battery Billions, Vestas in Scotland, and the Quiet Reshaping of Buildings Policy
item.description: A bumper Thursday for energy storage deals and clean energy manufacturing, with Brookfield snapping up Canada's biggest BESS developer and Vestas confirming a Scottish nacelle factory. Beneath the deal-flow, the policy architecture around buildings decarbonisation and electricity market design is quietly being redrawn on both sides of the Channel.
item.itunes:title: Battery Billions, Vestas in Scotland, and the Quiet Reshaping of Buildings Policy
item.itunes:summary: A bumper Thursday for energy storage deals and clean energy manufacturing, with Brookfield snapping up Canada's biggest BESS developer and Vestas confirming a Scottish nacelle factory. Beneath the deal-flow, the policy architecture around buildings decarbonisation and electricity market design is quietly being redrawn on both sides of the Channel.

item.title: The Quiet Rewiring of Britain: How Heat Pumps Are Reshaping the Grid From the Inside Out
item.description: Mass heat pump adoption isn't just a story about boilers and installers — it's forcing a fundamental rethink of how the UK's electricity network is planned, priced, and operated. The decisions being made right now, in 2026, will lock in either a virtuous cycle of falling costs and smart flexibility, or a vicious one of socialised grid upgrades and consumer backlash. Here's what's at stake.
item.itunes:title: The Quiet Rewiring of Britain: How Heat Pumps Are Reshaping the Grid From the Inside Out
item.itunes:summary: Mass heat pump adoption isn't just a story about boilers and installers — it's forcing a fundamental rethink of how the UK's electricity network is planned, priced, and operated. The decisions being made right now, in 2026, will lock in either a virtuous cycle of falling costs and smart flexibility, or a vicious one of socialised grid upgrades and consumer backlash. Here's what's at stake.

item.title: Storage Moves Centre Stage: From French Subsidies to AI Data Centres
item.description: Battery storage is asserting itself across every corner of the energy transition this week — reshaping subsidy design in France, powering AI ambitions in California, and setting new records in the US. Meanwhile, the UK quietly mandates heat pumps and solar for new homes, and perovskite technology edges closer to commercial reality in China.
item.itunes:title: Storage Moves Centre Stage: From French Subsidies to AI Data Centres
item.itunes:summary: Battery storage is asserting itself across every corner of the energy transition this week — reshaping subsidy design in France, powering AI ambitions in California, and setting new records in the US. Meanwhile, the UK quietly mandates heat pumps and solar for new homes, and perovskite technology edges closer to commercial reality in China.
